title: Module 08 — Results and Validation
problem: Starting from the provided Globe_Valve_SS08_Start.wbpz archive (solved Static Structural analysis), review and animate Total Deformation, add scoped Equivalent Stress results for the Valve Body and Flange, customize contour scaling/display, probe numerical values, create Reaction Force results for each Frictionless Support, check radial/tangential stress against thick-walled-cylinder theory using a local cylindrical coordinate system, compute a Safety Factor with a Mohr-Coulomb Stress Tool (147 MPa tensile/compressive limits), add Principal Stress results, run a Stress Convergence study on the Valve Body's outer surface (5% allowable change, up to 4 refinement loops, iterative solver), and finally generate and review a report.

_notes.source: Built from ANSYS Mechanical Getting Started, Release 2023 R1 — Module 08 Student Step-by-Step Guide: Results and Validation.
_notes.selector_status: UNVERIFIED — selectors use display names/labels taken from the source slide screenshots, following the same convention as tut1.json. Resolve real UIA automation_id/name via live testing before relying on them.
_notes.highlight_legend: uia = overlay draws a box around a UIA element (Outline tree items, ribbon buttons, Details fields). ocr_text = OCR-based highlight for the Workbench Toolbox / Project Schematic (not exposed to UIA — see tut1.json's workbench_uia_finding note). instruction = 3D-viewport/graphics-window action UIA cannot see (includes contour-legend drag/edit/reset, since the legend is drawn inside the graphics window, not a separate UIA-exposed control); shows a reference screenshot instead of a tracked box. none = no highlight (launch).
_notes.verify_legend: Solve and every 'Insert > result/tool' action use script(pymechanical)/uia checks (real, checkable model-state changes), matching tut1.json's pattern for solve/result steps. Field edits, contour-display toggles, probe interactions, and the final report preview use verify.type == "manual", per tut1's manual_verify_switch_2026-06 decision.
_notes.report_checks_rationale: This module ends with an explicit pass/fail design check (Safety Factor Min against the Mohr-Coulomb 147 MPa limits), so expected_result targets the converged Safety Factor minimum shown on slide 16 (0.91968), with a generous ±0.05 tolerance since the exact converged value depends on the Stress Convergence loop count and mesh state carried over from earlier modules — confirm by re-running live.
_notes.update_project_step: wb_03_update_project reflects slide 3's instruction to run Update Project (not just open the archive) so the model is already solved before opening Mechanical — unlike tut5-tut7, which open an unsolved starter file and solve later inside Mechanical.
_notes.wb_steps_reused: wb_01_open / wb_02_open_archive / wb_04_edit_model mirror the equivalent steps in tut5-tut7 (archive filename Globe_Valve_SS08_Start.wbpz differs); wb_03_update_project is the one addition specific to this module.
_notes.drag_drop_step: me_23_drag_frictionless_supports_to_solution uses action.kind 'drag' on Outline-tree items (Frictionless Support 1-4 onto Solution), the same tree-drag pattern as wb_03_add_static_structural's Toolbox drag in tut1.json, just within Mechanical's UIA-visible Outline tree instead of Workbench's non-UIA schematic.

sections.section: Open the starter project and solve it in Workbench

steps.title: Open Ansys Workbench
steps.description: Launch Ansys Workbench: Windows Start Menu button → All apps → ANSYS nn.n → Workbench nn.n.

verify.prompt: Is Ansys Workbench open?

hints: Start it from the Windows Start menu or the lab launcher.

steps.title: Open the starter archive
steps.description: File → Open…, browse for archive file Globe_Valve_SS08_Start.wbpz, and click Open.

verify.prompt: Project opened, showing Static Structural with Engineering Data/Geometry checked and Model/Setup/Solution/Results pending?

hints: Click File to open the menu, then choose Open…

steps.title: Update Project to solve the model
steps.description: Click Update Project in the toolbar so the model solves before opening Mechanical.

verify.prompt: Setup, Solution, and Results cells all show green checkmarks?

hints: Update Project is next to Refresh Project on the Project toolbar.

steps.title: Open Mechanical
steps.description: Right-click the Model cell in the Static Structural system block and choose Edit… to launch Ansys Mechanical.

verify.prompt: Mechanical opened, showing the already-solved Solution branch?

hints: The system block is in the Project Schematic; 'Model' is the row with the lightning-bolt icon.

sections.section: Review and animate the Total Deformation result

steps.title: Select Total Deformation
steps.description: Select Total Deformation in the Outline tree.

verify.prompt: Total Deformation contour showing in the Geometry view?

hints: Total Deformation is under Solution in the Outline tree.

steps.title: Play the deformation animation
steps.description: Click the Animation "Play" button in the Animation controller below the graphics window.

verify.prompt: Model animated through the 20-frame deformation cycle?

hints: The Animation controller is docked below the Geometry view once a result is selected.

sections.section: Add Equivalent Stress results for the Valve Body and Flange

steps.title: Review the overall Equivalent Stress plot
steps.description: Select Equivalent Stress in the Outline tree and review the plot.

verify.prompt: Overall Equivalent (von-Mises) Stress contour reviewed?

hints: This is the whole-assembly stress result already present from Module 07.

steps.title: Select the Solution branch
steps.description: Select Solution in the Outline tree.

verify.prompt: Solution branch selected?

hints: Solution sits above the existing result branches.

steps.title: Select the Valve Body in the graphics
steps.description: Select the Valve Body part in the Geometry view.

verify.prompt: Valve Body highlighted as the selected part?

hints: With the Body filter active, a single click selects the whole part.

steps.title: Insert Equivalent Stress scoped to the Valve Body
steps.description: Right-click → Insert → Stress → Equivalent, scoped to the selected Valve Body.
hints: This creates 'Equivalent Stress 2' under Solution.

steps.title: Select the Flange in the graphics
steps.description: Select the Flange part in the Geometry view.

verify.prompt: Flange highlighted as the selected part?

hints: Click one of the two flange parts on either end of the valve.

steps.title: Insert Equivalent Stress scoped to the Flange
steps.description: Right-click → Insert → Stress → Equivalent, scoped to the selected Flange.
hints: This creates 'Equivalent Stress 3' under Solution.

steps.title: Evaluate all results
steps.description: Right-click Solution and choose Evaluate All Results.
hints: Both new scoped stress results evaluate together.

steps.title: Select any stress result plot
steps.description: Select any of the Equivalent Stress results in the Outline tree.

verify.prompt: A stress contour plot with its legend showing?

hints: Any Equivalent Stress branch works for this exercise.

steps.title: Drag a contour-legend divider line
steps.description: Click and drag a line on the contour legend that separates two adjacent colors.

verify.prompt: Contour band boundary moved by dragging?

hints: The divider lines sit between the color bands on the legend, left of the Geometry view.

steps.title: Set a contour legend value to 100
steps.description: Right-click one of the existing contour legend values and choose Edit, then type 100.

verify.prompt: One of the legend bands now reads exactly 100?

hints: Right-click directly on a numeric value in the legend, not the color swatch.

steps.title: Add more contour colors
steps.description: Click on the legend, then use + / - to add contour colors/bands.

verify.prompt: Number of contour bands increased using +?

hints: The +/- controls appear when the legend is active/selected.

steps.title: Reset the contour legend
steps.description: Right-click on the Contour Legend and choose Reset All to restore the automatic legend.

verify.prompt: Legend back to automatic values (e.g. 136.06 Max)?

hints: 'Reset All' is at the bottom of the legend's right-click menu.

sections.section: Adjust deformed shape scale and contour display

steps.title: Select any stress result plot
steps.description: Select any Equivalent Stress result in the Outline tree.

verify.prompt: Result Toolbar visible above the Geometry view?

hints: The Result Toolbar appears when a result branch is selected.

steps.title: Adjust the deformed shape scale
steps.description: Use the displacement scaling dropdown on the Result Toolbar (e.g. Undeformed, True Scale, Auto Scale, 2x Auto, 5x Auto) to compare deformed shapes.

verify.prompt: Deformed shape visibly changed scale (e.g. between Undeformed and 5x Auto)?

hints: The scale dropdown is at the left end of the Result Toolbar.

steps.title: Toggle Smooth / Banded contours
steps.description: Use the Contours dropdown on the Result Toolbar to switch between Smooth Contours, Contour Bands, Isolines, and Solid Fill.

verify.prompt: Contour style visibly changed between Smooth and Banded?

hints: The Contours dropdown is next to Geometry on the Result Toolbar.

steps.title: Show element outlines and undeformed geometry
steps.description: Use the Edges dropdown to try Show Elements, Show Undeformed Model, and Show Undeformed WireFrame.

verify.prompt: Element outlines and/or undeformed wireframe visible overlaid on the contour?

hints: The Edges dropdown is next to Contours on the Result Toolbar.

sections.section: Use a Probe to read numerical values

steps.title: Select any stress result plot
steps.description: Select any Equivalent Stress result in the Outline tree.

verify.prompt: Stress contour and Result Toolbar showing?

hints: Any Equivalent Stress branch works.

steps.title: Activate the Probe tool
steps.description: Click the Probe icon on the Result Toolbar.

verify.prompt: Probe cursor mode active?

hints: Probe is at the right end of the Result Toolbar, next to Maximum/Minimum.

steps.title: Click a location on the contour plot
steps.description: Click a desired location on the contour plot to place a numeric-value annotation.

verify.prompt: A Result annotation with a numeric value appeared at the clicked location?

hints: Repeat for several locations to compare values, e.g. near stress concentrations.

steps.title: Delete all Probe annotations
steps.description: In the Graphics Annotations window, right-click a Result row and choose Delete All.

verify.prompt: All probe annotations removed from Graphics Annotations and the graphics view?

hints: Graphics Annotations is a dockable panel; right-click any row in it.

sections.section: Create Reaction Force results for each support

steps.title: Drag each Frictionless Support onto Solution
steps.description: In the Outline tree, drag/drop each of the 4 Frictionless Support branches onto the Solution branch to create a Force Reaction result for each.

verify.prompt: Force Reaction, Force Reaction 2, Force Reaction 3, and Force Reaction 4 all created under Solution?

hints: Ctrl+select all 4 Frictionless Support branches, then drag them together onto Solution.

steps.title: Evaluate all results
steps.description: Right-click Solution and choose Evaluate All Results.
hints: All 4 Force Reaction results evaluate together.

steps.title: Review each Force Reaction
steps.description: Select, in turn, Force Reaction, Force Reaction 2, Force Reaction 3, and Force Reaction 4 and review each for magnitude and direction.

verify.prompt: All 4 reaction force arrows (magnitude + direction) reviewed on their respective flange/hole faces?

hints: The reaction arrow direction should make physical sense given the pressure load direction.

sections.section: Check radial and tangential stress against thick-walled-cylinder theory

steps.title: Insert Normal Stress on the inside surface
steps.description: Select the inside cylindrical surface of the Valve Body's straight section, then right-click Solution → Insert → Stress → Normal.
hints: This creates 'Normal Stress' under Solution, scoped to 1 Face.

steps.title: Insert Normal Stress on the outside surface
steps.description: Select the outside cylindrical surface of the Valve Body's straight section, then right-click Solution → Insert → Stress → Normal.
hints: This creates 'Normal Stress 2' under Solution, scoped to 1 Face.

steps.title: Create a local cylindrical coordinate system
steps.description: Insert a new Coordinate System, set its Type to Cylindrical, and define its origin by clicking the outside surface of the Valve Body (Origin defined by Geometry Selection).

verify.prompt: New Coordinate System shows Type = Cylindrical with Origin defined on the Valve Body's outside surface?

hints: Set Type to Cylindrical in Details, then click the outer surface for Origin > Geometry.

steps.title: Reference the cylindrical CS in Normal Stress
steps.description: Select Normal Stress, and in its Details set Coordinate System to the new local cylindrical Coordinate System, and Orientation to X Axis (radial).

verify.prompt: Normal Stress now references the local cylindrical coordinate system?

hints: Coordinate System and Orientation are both in the Definition section of Details.

steps.title: Reference the cylindrical CS in Normal Stress 2
steps.description: Select Normal Stress 2, and in its Details set Coordinate System to the new local cylindrical Coordinate System, and Orientation as needed (X = radial, Y = tangential).

verify.prompt: Normal Stress 2 now references the local cylindrical coordinate system?

hints: Repeat the same Details change made for Normal Stress.

steps.title: Evaluate all results
steps.description: Right-click Solution and choose Evaluate All Results.
hints: Both Normal Stress results evaluate together.

steps.title: Probe and compare against thick-walled-cylinder theory
steps.description: Use Probe on the inside/outside Normal Stress plots to read radial (X component) and tangential (Y component) stress values, and compare against theoretical thick-walled-cylinder formulas.

verify.prompt: Probed radial/tangential values compared to the theoretical thick-walled-cylinder formulas and found reasonably close?

hints: Radial stress should be near zero on the outside (free surface) and near -internal pressure on the inside.

sections.section: Calculate a Safety Factor with a Stress Tool

steps.title: Insert a Stress Tool
steps.description: Right-click Solution → Insert → Stress Tool → Mohr-Coulomb Stress.
hints: This creates a 'Stress Tool' branch with a 'Safety Factor' child under Solution.

steps.title: Set Theory to Mohr-Coulomb
steps.description: In the Stress Tool Details, confirm/set Theory to Mohr-Coulomb Stress.

verify.prompt: Theory shows Mohr-Coulomb Stress?

hints: Theory is in the Definition section of the Stress Tool Details.

steps.title: Set Tensile Limit Type to Custom
steps.description: Change Tensile Limit Type to Custom Value.

verify.prompt: Tensile Limit Type shows Custom Value, revealing a Tensile Limit field?

hints: This is right below Theory in the Definition section.

steps.title: Set Compressive Limit Type to Custom
steps.description: Change Compressive Limit Type to Custom Value.

verify.prompt: Compressive Limit Type shows Custom Value, revealing a Compressive Limit field?

hints: Right below Tensile Limit Type.

steps.title: Set both limits to 147 MPa
steps.description: Set both Tensile Limit and Compressive Limit to 147 MPa (half of the 294 MPa ultimate strength).

verify.prompt: Both Tensile Limit and Compressive Limit read 147 MPa?

hints: Repeat the same value entry for Compressive Limit.

steps.title: Scope Safety Factor to the Valve Body
steps.description: Select the Safety Factor result under Stress Tool and change its scoping (Geometry) to the Valve Body part.

verify.prompt: Safety Factor's Geometry now scoped to only the Valve Body part?

hints: Geometry is in the Scope section of the Safety Factor Details.

steps.title: Evaluate all results
steps.description: Right-click Solution and choose Evaluate All Results.
hints: Safety Factor evaluates using the 147 MPa Mohr-Coulomb limits.

sections.section: Add Principal Stress results for the Valve Body

steps.title: Insert Maximum Principal Stress
steps.description: Select the Valve Body, then right-click Solution → Insert → Stress → Maximum Principal.
hints: Select the Valve Body part before inserting so the result scopes to it.

steps.title: Insert Middle Principal Stress
steps.description: With the Valve Body still selected, right-click Solution → Insert → Stress → Middle Principal.
hints: Same right-click menu, choose Middle Principal this time.

steps.title: Insert Minimum Principal Stress
steps.description: With the Valve Body still selected, right-click Solution → Insert → Stress → Minimum Principal.
hints: Same right-click menu, choose Minimum Principal.

steps.title: Evaluate all results
steps.description: Right-click Solution and choose Evaluate All Results.
hints: All three principal stress results evaluate together.

steps.title: Check contour smoothness with element outlines
steps.description: With element outlines turned on (Edges → Show Elements), review whether the contour bands are smooth in the critical regions of minimum safety factor.

verify.prompt: Contour bands reviewed with element outlines on — are they smooth (well-converged) in the critical region, or jagged/mesh-dependent?

hints: Jagged, element-sized contour bands in the hot spot suggest the mesh isn't fine enough there yet.

steps.title: Insert Equivalent Stress on the Valve Body's outer surfaces
steps.description: Select one outer surface of the Valve Body, use Extend to Limits to grow the selection to all connected outer surfaces, then right-click Solution → Insert → Stress → Equivalent.
hints: Extend to Limits is on the Selection toolbar; it grows a single-face pick to the whole connected outer shell (63 faces in the source example).

steps.title: Evaluate all results
steps.description: Right-click Solution and choose Evaluate All Results.
hints: The new outer-surface Equivalent Stress result evaluates along with the rest.

steps.title: Insert a Convergence check
steps.description: Right-click the newly created Equivalent Stress result (outer-surface scoped) → Insert → Convergence.
hints: Right-click the specific Equivalent Stress branch scoped to the Valve Body's outer surfaces, not the whole-body one.

steps.title: Set Allowable Change to 5%
steps.description: In the Convergence Details, set Allowable Change to 5%.

verify.prompt: Allowable Change shows 5. %?

hints: Allowable Change is in the Definition section of the Convergence Details.

steps.title: Set Max Refinement Loops to 4
steps.description: Select Solution and, in Details → Adaptive Mesh Refinement, set Max Refinement Loops to 4.

verify.prompt: Max Refinement Loops shows 4?

hints: Adaptive Mesh Refinement is a section of the Solution Details, not the Convergence Details.

sections.section: Switch to a more efficient solver and re-solve

steps.title: Set Solver Type to Iterative
steps.description: Select Analysis Settings and, in Details → Solver Controls, set Solver Type to Iterative (more efficient for this linear problem).

verify.prompt: Solver Type shows Iterative?

hints: Solver Type is in the Solver Controls section of Analysis Settings Details.

steps.title: Solve
steps.description: Click Solve on the Home ribbon to re-solve with adaptive mesh refinement driven by the new Convergence criterion.
hints: This may take longer than earlier solves since it can run up to 4 refinement loops.

sections.section: Review convergence and the final Safety Factor

steps.title: Review the Convergence History
steps.description: Click on Convergence (under the outer-surface Equivalent Stress result) and review its Convergence History chart/table.

verify.prompt: Convergence chart shows Last Change under the 5% Allowable Change and Converged = Yes?

hints: The chart plots Equivalent Stress vs. Solution Number across refinement loops.

steps.title: View the converged Valve Body stress
steps.description: View the Valve Body equivalent stress contour with element outlines turned on, to confirm the contour is now smooth after refinement.

verify.prompt: Contour bands in the previously-jagged critical region now smooth after mesh refinement?

hints: Compare this view to me_44's earlier, coarser-mesh contour.

steps.title: View the final Safety Factor result
steps.description: Select the Safety Factor result and review its Min value. Has the design criteria been met (Min > 1)?

verify.prompt: Safety Factor Min reviewed (approximately 0.92) — design criteria (Min > 1) NOT met; note where the minimum occurs.

hints: Expected result from the source guide: Safety Factor Min ≈ 0.91968, below the target of 1.0.

steps.title: Insert a Figure on Geometry
steps.description: Select Geometry in the Outline tree, then Home ribbon → Images → Figure to capture it for the report.

verify.prompt: Figure inserted under Geometry?

hints: Images is in the Insert group of the Home ribbon.

steps.title: Insert a Figure on Mesh
steps.description: Select Mesh in the Outline tree, then Home ribbon → Images → Figure.

verify.prompt: Figure inserted under Mesh?

hints: Same Images > Figure control, with Mesh selected instead.

steps.title: Insert a Figure on Static Structural
steps.description: Select Static Structural (the environment branch) in the Outline tree, then Home ribbon → Images → Figure.

verify.prompt: Figure inserted under Static Structural, capturing the loads/supports view?

hints: This captures the same annotated loads/supports view reviewed back in Module 07.

steps.title: Insert a Figure on Total Deformation
steps.description: Select Total Deformation, then Home ribbon → Images → Figure.

verify.prompt: Figure inserted under Total Deformation?

hints: Same Images > Figure control.

steps.title: Insert a Figure on the converged Valve Body stress
steps.description: Select the converged, outer-surface-scoped Equivalent Stress result (with the Convergence child), then Home ribbon → Images → Figure.

verify.prompt: Figure inserted under the converged Equivalent Stress result?

hints: This is the result created in me_45, not the original whole-assembly Equivalent Stress.

steps.title: Insert a Figure on Safety Factor
steps.description: Select the Safety Factor result, then Home ribbon → Images → Figure.

verify.prompt: Figure inserted under Safety Factor?

hints: Same Images > Figure control.

steps.title: Preview the report
steps.description: Click Report Preview on the Home tab to generate and review the full report.

verify.prompt: Report generated, including all inserted Figures and their captions?

hints: Report Preview is in the Tools group of the Home ribbon.
